title: Valsalva Manoeuvre
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- To transiently slow and revert supraventricular tachycardia (SVT) and other narrow complex tachycardias.

#### Procedure

- Confirm SVT via a 12 lead ECG. Confirm with partner if possible.

- Monitor ECG throughout procedure. 
- If possible, have IV access already established.

- Position patient in a semi recumbent (45\xb0) position.

- Inform the patient to attempt to blow a 3ml or 10ml syringe plunger out in a “bearing down” motion for at least 10 seconds (see **Figure 1**)

Figure 1

- At the completion of the “bearing down” period posture patient flat with legs raised to 45\xb0 for at least 15 seconds. Patient can then be returned to the semi-recumbent position.

- Ensure a rhythm strip is recorded throughout the attempt.

- Repeat procedure **once** if there was no change to the rhythm or rate.

#### Contraindications

- Nil when indicated.

#### Specific Notes

- 
- A patient in SVT or other narrow complex tachycardia must be monitored at all times.
